seoTitle: How to Use Huddles on Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Start an instant audio or video huddle in Slack, share your screen, use live captions, and collaborate on a huddle canvas.
intro: Gathering your team for a spontaneous sync shouldn't require scheduling a formal meeting link. This guide covers starting a Slack huddle, enabling video and captions, sharing your screen, and using the collaborative huddle canvas.

steps.heading: Start a huddle
steps.body: In any channel or direct message, click the headphones icon in the lower left to begin an instant audio session.

steps.heading: Turn on your camera
steps.body: Click the camera icon in the floating call tray to add your live video feed.

steps.heading: Enable captions
steps.body: Click the three-dot menu and select 'Turn on Captions' for real-time voice-to-text tracking.

steps.heading: Share your screen
steps.body: Click the Share Screen option to choose a specific window or your entire monitor to display to the group.

steps.heading: Use the huddle canvas
steps.body: Click the canvas icon to open a side-by-side notepad for collaborative checklists and notes during the call.

faq.question: Can I invite someone to a huddle who isn't in the channel?
faq.answer: Yes, click the plus person icon to directly ping and invite specific teammates into the active discussion.

faq.question: Is there a participant limit for a huddle?
faq.answer: Huddles support multiple participants, though very large groups may be better suited for a scheduled video call.

faq.question: Do huddles get recorded automatically?
faq.answer: No, huddles aren't recorded by default, so you'd need a separate recording tool if that's needed.

faq.question: How do I leave a huddle?
faq.answer: Click the headphones toggle a second time to cleanly disconnect and exit the session.
